summary_one_line: A three-part dotted name (schema.table.column) so the parser sets zDb non-NULL and lookupName takes the if(zDb) true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: SQL text that parses as a three-part qualified name 'X.Y.Z' (two dots) reaching the TK_DOT case in resolveExprStep, so lookupName is called with zDb != 0; reaching the true side of `if( zDb )` at line 97837.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed is 'SELECT M.L. .L.4 ...' with multiple '.' (0x2e) separators producing dotted X.Y.Z tokens; per-role overlay shows the winner alone hits lines 97838-97861 (the zDb body) and line 98387 lookupName call after the TK_DOT path, and the blocker count shows W T=1 vs L T=0 at L97837.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are 'SELECT CC........' / 'SELECT+SSE++++++' with no two-dot qualified-name structure, so the parser yields TK_ID (or no dotted name) and lookupName is called with zDb==0; the overlay marks the zDb-body lines as not-hit for L and the blocker shows L T=0 F=20.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose CMP operands get closer to producing the dotted-name token structure: each near-match on the byte comparisons in the tokenizer/parser adds a new CMP_MAP distance bucket, preserving partial-progress seeds so the rare two-dot qualified-name layout is assembled incrementally. Naive/cmplog (without CMP_MAP) lack this gradient and only rarely stumble onto the structure via havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the true branch of `if( zDb )` in lookupName at sqlite3.c:97837.
Both decisive pairs are won by the value_profile-adding fuzzer, delta = value_profile.
The second decisive pair adds value_profile on top of cmplog.
zDb originates from the TK_DOT parsing path that calls lookupName with a database/table qualifier.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: return lookupName(pParse, zDb, zTable, zColumn, pNC, pExpr); <-- CALL

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving seed contains dotted qualified-name structure (multiple '.' separators) consistent with X.Y.Z.
The loser-blocking seed lacks the dotted qualified-name structure.
Only the winner takes the zDb-body lines while the loser does not, and the blocker itself shows W true vs L false.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L97837  T=1 F=0  T=0 F=20  if( zDb ){  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile preserves an input when its CMP operand pair is closer to matching than any previously seen, via a new CMP_MAP distance bucket.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile encodes operand closeness as a distance bucket (Hamming distance / matching-prefix length).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The distance bucket is a coarse encoding of how close the two
operands were (Hamming distance bucket for `trace_cmp*`; matching-prefix
length for string/memory CMPs).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile delta), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage I2S-floor check does not apply; the value_profile winner has no I2S mutator stage and the dash-row floor signal would not be the relevant evidence here.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on `if(zDb)` reached only via a two-dot qualified name shows value_profile NOT outperforming naive (no separation across the dose-response sweep on the dotted-structure knob), indicating CMP_MAP gradient is not what assembles the qualified name.

weakest_evidence_point: The single winner-resolving seed is from value_profile trial 1 and its mutation_op is havoc operators (QwordAddMutator,DwordAddMutator,BytesCopyMutator), so the CMP_MAP gradient role is inferred from the value_profile feedback mechanism and corpus-preservation rather than directly observed in the seed's own mutator chain.
